page: Google Cloud Platform m3-ultramem-128 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for memory-intensive workloads. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 64 physical Intel Xeon cores using x86_64 architecture with hyperthreading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz and includes a 54 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 3904.0 GB of RAM, providing a high resource density of 30.5 GB of memory per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance alongside moderate single-core capabilities. The instance achieved a top-tier Passmark CPU score of 83553.05 and a strong stress-ng multi-core CPU performance rating. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 298.34 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 123.31 GB/sec for uncached writes. However, single-core performance is average, as shown by the stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores. Additionally, while prompt processing for small and medium LLMs is strong, text generation for large 70B models is a notable bottleneck, yielding a weak result of 2.73 tokens/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m3-ultramem-128 offers a highly specialized hardware profile that trades off local storage and GPU acceleration to maximize memory capacity per vCPU. This makes it an efficient allocation for massive in-memory databases, large-scale Redis deployments, and complex data processing tasks that fit entirely within system memory. It is also well-suited for multi-threaded enterprise applications, software compilation, and high-throughput static web serving, though it is less optimal for workloads requiring raw single-threaded speed or heavy GPU-bound machine learning training.
description: Google Cloud Platform m3-ultramem-128 is a memory-optimized server configured with 128 dedicated vCPUs on 64 physical Intel Xeon cores and 3904.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.6 GHz, this instance delivers 30.5 GB of memory per core without bundled local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show top-tier multi-core execution and strong memory bandwidth, with uncached reads reaching 298.34 GB/sec and Redis operations exceeding 106 million operations per second. Conversely, single-core performance and large-model LLM text generation remain average to weak. This profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for massive in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and memory-bound enterprise workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m3-ultramem-128 is a memory-optimized server featuring 128 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 3904 GB of RAM, designed for massive in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m3-ultramem-128 features 128 vCPUs and 3904 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for in-memory databases.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized platform featuring massive RAM capacity and strong multi-core performance for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated vCPUs and 64 physical Intel Xeon cores at 2.6 GHz
3904.0 GB of RAM providing 30.5 GB of memory per core
Strong memory bandwidth with 298.34 GB/sec uncached read speeds
Top-tier multi-core performance and high-throughput Redis operations
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for massive in-memory databases and memory-bound enterprise applications
